data.title: Cogeco Communications reported Q1 2026 results with $707.2m revenue, $353.8m EBITDA, and $93.1m profit. Positive changes in U.S. subscriber trends. Network upgrades include 2.5 Gigabit speeds in the U.S. New digital brand to launch in the U.S. Credit outlooks upgraded by S&P and Moody's. Dividend increased by 7.0% to $0.987 per share.
data.description: Cogeco Communications Inc. announced its financial results for the first quarter ended November 30, 2025. Revenue decreased by 4.3% to $707.2 million, with adjusted EBITDA dropping by 3.1% to $353.8 million. Profit for the period was $93.1 million. In the U.S., subscriber trends improved, leading to the best metrics in 15 quarters. Canadian customer growth continued, with network upgrades ongoing. Credit outlooks by S&P and Moody's improved, and fiscal 2026 financial guidelines were re-confirmed. Dividend per share increased by 7.0% to $0.987. Network expansion saw over 4,000 homes passed in Q1, with capital intensity at 22.2%. Acquisition of property, plant, and equipment increased by 2.6% to $157.2 million, while free cash flow decreased by 15.7% to $125.5 million. Operating activities cash flow decreased by 19.4% to $176.3 million. These changes were seen across revenue, adjusted EBITDA, profit, cash flow, and capital expenditures, impacting the financial performance of Cogeco Communications Inc.

data.title: Cogeco reports Q1 2026 revenue of $735.6M, down 3.8%; Adjusted EBITDA at $361.8M, a 2.5% decrease; Profit at $96.1M.
data.description: Cogeco Inc. announced its financial results for the first quarter ended November 30, 2025. The company reported revenue of $735.6 million, a 3.8% decrease from the previous year. Adjusted EBITDA decreased by 2.5% to $361.8 million. In the U.S., subscriber trends improved, leading to the best metrics in 15 quarters. Canadian customer base continued to grow, with network upgrades ongoing. Cogeco Media saw revenue growth despite challenges in the radio market. Net capital expenditures increased to $157.2 million, with network expansion projects continuing. The Board declared a quarterly dividend of $0.987 per share. Credit outlooks from S&P and Moody's improved. Cash flows from operating activities decreased by 16.3% to $174.6 million. Overall, the company showed resilience and growth despite market challenges.

data.title: Alcott Global now accepting nominations for 2026 Leaders in Supply Chain Awards; recognizing executives shaping global supply chains.
data.description: In Singapore on January 14, 2026, the "Leaders in Supply Chain Awards" for the year 2026 have been launched by Alcott Global. This event is dedicated to recognizing executives driving significant changes in global supply chains. The theme for this year is "Connecting the Dots", emphasizing the importance of coordination and collaboration in supply chain operations. This annual platform brings attention to the achievements of leaders in this field.

data.title: J&T Global Express and SF Holding cross-shareholding partnership to create global smart logistics ecosystem. (Total of 1519.HK, 002352.SZ, 6936.HK)
data.description: Today, in Hong Kong on January 14, 2026, J&T Global Express (1519.HK) and S.F. Holding Co., Ltd. (002352.SZ, 6936.HK) revealed a strategic partnership. J&T Express is a prominent integrated logistics service provider globally, and SF Holding is a notable player in the industry.
The yield on the 20-year Japanese government bond fell 2 basis points to 3.140%.
China A50 index futures slipped 0.07% as trading commenced.

data.title: Woodside signs contract with JERA to supply 3 LNG cargoes (total 0.2 million tons/year) annually starting 2027 for 5 years.
data.description: In Perth, Australia, Woodside Energy Trading Singapore Pte Ltd (Woodside) has finalized a Sale and Purchase Agreement (SPA) with JERA Co., Inc. for the provision of three liquefied natural gas (LNG) cargoes annually, totaling around 0.2 million tonnes per year. This deal is set for a duration of five years starting in 2027. The LNG shipments will be delivered on a delivered ex-ship (DES) basis to Japan specifically throughout the peak winter season, spanning from December to February.
Taiwan’s overnight interbank rate held at 0.805%, signaling unchanged short-term liquidity conditions.
